senses.definition: the state or practice of being a scrivener or writing, especially the role of a lawyer's clerk or scribe in handling documents
examples: He studied scrivenership as part of his legal training.
The guild valued scrivenership for its careful record-keeping.

senses.definition: plural of scrivenership; multiple instances or periods of being a scrivener or performing writing/record-keeping duties
examples: Various scrivenerships were documented in the old ledgers.
During the era, scrivenerships were common among clerks.

senses.definition: the art or practice of writing, especially in a legal or official context; a place where writing is done, such as a scribe’s workshop
examples: The contract was drafted in scrivenery under careful supervision.
He learned scrivenery in the clerk’s office.

senses.definition: the act of writing or copying documents, especially by a scrivener; to draft or copy official texts
examples: The clerk spent the afternoon scrivening the deed.
She was trained in scrivening and archival skills.

senses.definition: the writings or documents produced by a scrivener; the process or products of scrivening
examples: Her scrivenings filled several ledgers over the year.
The archive preserved many old scrivenings.

senses.definition: in the manner of a scrivener; with careful, meticulous writing
examples: He checked the contract scrivenly before submitting it.
The clerk wrote scrivenly to avoid mistakes.

senses.definition: a writer or scribe; someone who writes or copies texts
examples: The old manuscript was in the hand of a skilled scriver.
A scriver transcribed the records during the ceremony.

senses.definition: plural form referring to writings or scribal works by a scriver
examples: The ancient scrolls varied in scrives.
Her scrives were cataloged in the library.

senses.definition: the act of writing; the practice of copying or drafting texts
examples: The commission required meticulous scriving.
Scriving was a valued skill in medieval offices.

senses.definition: a short, sudden movement; a scrobbing may also refer to a rapid, light touch or scrape
examples: With a quick scrob, the feather brushed past.
He gave the device a scrob to test its sensitivity.

senses.definition: past tense of scrob; moved or touched with a quick, light motion
examples: He scrobbled the edge to remove dust.
The device scrobbbed softly against the cloth.

senses.definition: present participle of scrob; making a quick, light movement
examples: She is scrobbing the keyboard with quick keystrokes.
The sensor scrobbing helps detect motion.

senses.definition: to touch or move something with a quick, light scrob; to record data by such motion
examples: The user scrobbles the button to start.
The device can scrobble the surface to log texture.

senses.definition: past tense of scrobble; made a quick, light movement or recorded such movement
examples: He scrobbled the screen to wake it up.
The data was scrobbled and saved.

senses.definition: plural of scrobble; quick, light touches or movements
examples: The screen performed several scrobbles as the sensor tested.
Her scrobbles across the surface left tiny marks.

senses.definition: present participle of scrobble; performing quick, light touches or logging such motions
examples: The device is scrobbling data in real time.
Users were scrobbling the screen to test sensitivity.

senses.definition: a small scrob; a tiny hollow or cavity on a surface
examples: A scrobe appeared in the metal panel.
The instrument measured the scrobe depth.

senses.definition: plural of scrobe; multiple small hollows or cavities
examples: The rock contained many scrobes.
Researchers cataloged the scrobes in the sample.

senses.definition: a small, rounded structure or form in biology; a diminutive of scrobus in some contexts
examples: The specimen showed a pronounced scrobicula.
Biologists noted the scrobicula near the pore openings.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling a scrobicle/scrobicula; curved or groove-like
examples: The scrobicular margin resembles a series of little grooves.
Anatomists described the scrobicular structure.

senses.definition: to form or mark with small grooves or pits; to groove
examples: The leaf surface is scrobiculate, aiding moisture control.
The fabric was scrobiculate, providing texture.

senses.definition: having small grooves or pits; grooved
examples: A scrobiculated surface helps with grip.
The shell is scrobiculated and rough to the touch.

senses.definition: a small scrobicle or tiny groove; a small pit or groove
examples: The scrobicule is barely visible to the naked eye.
Researchers noted the scrobicule along the limb.

senses.definition: plural of scrobicule; multiple small grooves or pits
examples: The insect’s exoskeleton has several scrobicules.
Microscopic slides show numerous scrobicules.

senses.definition: a small depression or groove; a diminutive anatomical groove
examples: The scrobiculus marks the attachment site.
Anatomists described the scrobiculus with care.
